reforms.description: The Czech Republic's government has proposed the Big Bang programme, aimed at enhancing economic growth in high-unemployment regions, supporting small and medium enterprises, and revitalising insolvent industrial firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The programme is designed to stimulate economic growth and support SMEs, which are crucial for job creation and economic resilience.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been proposed by the government but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Big Bang programme represents a significant structural change aimed at revitalising the economy in targeted regions.
reforms.source_quote: The programme, which has yet to be approved, seeks to significantly improve economic conditions in 13 high-unemployment regions; to support small and medium enterprises; to enhance the domestic manufacturing sector’s performance.

reforms.description: The Czech government introduced an investment incentives scheme in April 1998, initially targeting the manufacturing sector and later extending it to IT areas such as software development and client support centres, supporting 41 new investment projects valued at over $2.3 billion.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The scheme has successfully attracted significant investment, fostering growth in key sectors and creating jobs.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been in effect since its introduction in 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the scheme is an extension of existing policies rather than a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s investment in incentives scheme, launched in April 1998... has certainly played a role in the pickup of FDI.
